task_completion.description: Page 1 shows a system architecture diagram with all required components: load balancer, 3 web applications (Patient Portal, Provider Dashboard, Admin Console), central API Gateway, 4 backend microservices (Authentication Service, EHR Records Service, Billing Service, Analytics Service), 2 databases (PostgreSQL, MongoDB), and HL7 FHIR Gateway cloud shape
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for labeled boxes or shapes for each of the named components arranged in a logical tiered layout. The load balancer should be at the top, web applications below it, API Gateway in the center, microservices below that, and databases at the bottom. The HL7 FHIR Gateway should appear as a cloud shape connected to EHR Records Service.

task_completion.description: Each component on Page 1 includes its technology stack label
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for technology annotations inside or beneath each component shape. Expected labels include: React/Nginx, Angular/Nginx, Vue.js/Nginx, Kong, Node.js, Java/Spring Boot, Python/Django, Go. At least 5 of 8 technology labels should be visible.

task_completion.description: Components on Page 1 are connected with directional arrows showing the architecture flow
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for connector lines or arrows between components forming a coherent architecture topology: load balancer to web apps, web apps to API Gateway, API Gateway to microservices, microservices to databases, EHR Records to FHIR Gateway. Components should not appear as isolated, unconnected shapes.

task_completion.description: Page 2 contains a cross-functional swimlane flowchart with 3 labeled lanes: Front Desk, Clinical Staff, and Billing Department
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for horizontal or vertical swimlanes with headers labeled 'Front Desk', 'Clinical Staff', and 'Billing Department'. Each lane should contain process shapes, and the overall structure should be a cross-functional diagram, not a simple flowchart without lanes.

task_completion.description: Page 2 flowchart includes at least 12 process steps and 3 decision diamonds with the specified labels, flowing from 'Patient Arrives' to 'Admission Complete'
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an oval or rounded start shape labeled 'Patient Arrives', diamond-shaped decision points with text 'Insurance Verified?', 'Pre-authorization Required?', and 'Room Available?', rectangular process boxes for steps like registration, triage, assessment, and room assignment, and an end shape labeled 'Admission Complete'. All shapes should be connected with directional arrows.

task_completion.description: Document was saved as .eddx and Page 1 was exported as PNG
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a File > Save As dialog or the application title bar displaying 'clearmed_architecture.eddx'. Also look for an Export dialog showing PNG format selection for exporting Page 1, or evidence that both save and export operations were performed.

integrity.description: The agent used the Wondershare EdrawMax GUI to create the diagram
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for screenshots showing the EdrawMax application open with the diagram being actively edited on the canvas, rather than terminal commands generating an XML/EDDX file directly.

integrity.description: The agent used actual diagramming shapes rather than pasting a single static image
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for selection handles around individual shapes, text boxes, or connectors, indicating the diagram was built using EdrawMax's native tools.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the application by creating a dummy file via terminal
integrity.visual_evidence: Ensure there are no terminal screenshots showing commands like 'touch /home/ga/Documents/clearmed_architecture.eddx' or script-based file creation without actual EdrawMax usage.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The output file must be saved exactly as '/home/ga/Documents/clearmed_architecture.eddx' and Page 1 exported as '/home/ga/Documents/clearmed_architecture.png'. The document must contain at least 2 pages. Page 1 must depict a multi-tier system architecture with these 14 components: Load Balancer, Patient Portal (React/Nginx), Provider Dashboard (Angular/Nginx), Admin Console (Vue.js/Nginx), API Gateway (Kong), Authentication Service (Node.js), EHR Records Service (Java/Spring Boot), Billing Service (Python/Django), Analytics Service (Go), PostgreSQL, MongoDB, HL7 FHIR Gateway. Page 2 must depict a cross-functional swimlane flowchart with 3 lanes (Front Desk, Clinical Staff, Billing Department), starting at 'Patient Arrives' and ending at 'Admission Complete', with 3 decision diamonds: 'Insurance Verified?', 'Pre-authorization Required?', 'Room Available?'.
